summary: Bryan Cantrill announces his two DTrace presentations at OSCON 2005. The first is a free hands-on tutorial covering OpenSolaris development with DTrace, while the second is a shorter session focusing on DTrace fundamentals and its relationship with open source projects. He invites attendees to connect with him during the conference.
key_points: Free tutorial on OpenSolaris development with DTrace on Tuesday
Demo-intensive introduction to DTrace and related tooling
Thursday session focuses on DTrace and open source intersection
Open invitation for conference attendees to connect
money_quotes: We're going to keep it very hands-on, and it will be a demo-intensive introduction to both DTrace and to our larger tool-set that we use to both build OpenSolaris and to diagnose it when it all goes horribly wrong.

This presentation is going to give a quick review of the DTrace fundamentals, and then focus on the confluence of DTrace and open source – both what DTrace can do for your open source project, and what you can do (if you're so inclined) for the DTrace open source project.
key_insight: DTrace represents a bidirectional opportunity for open source: it can help developers understand their projects while also benefiting from community contributions.
